acquisition method: we performed a label-free mass spectrometry (MS)–based analysis of protein ubiquitination
alkylation reagent: alkylated with 11 mM iodoacetamide for 15 min at room temperature in darkness
alkylation concentration: alkylated with 11 mM iodoacetamide for 15 min at room temperature in darkness
cleavage agent: trypsin was added at 1:50 trypsin-to-protein-mass ratio for the first digestion overnight
collision energy: the fragments were detected in the Orbitrap at a resolution of 17,500 with NCE setting of 28
enrichment method: using the di-Gly-Lys–remnant antibody enrichment approach using 6-wk-old upl3 and WT plants
fractionation method: the tryptic peptides were fractionated by high-pH reverse-phase HPLC
fragmentation method: mass spectrometry analysis with high-energy collisional dissociation quantum efficiency mass spectrometry (QE-MS)
instrument: the peptides were subjected to NSI source followed by tandem mass spectrometry (MS/MS) in Q Exactive TM Plus
labeling: we performed a label-free mass spectrometry (MS)–based analysis of protein ubiquitination
reduction reagent: 10 mM dithiothreitol, and 1% Protease Inhibitor Cocktail [Roche]
reduction concentration: A total of 5 mM dithiothreitol was added to the protein solution for reduction
